question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTransactionTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a large-volume token freeze transaction batch that causes the @Transactional(timeoutString = '#{@recordParserProperties.getTransactionTimeout().toSeconds()}') deadline to expire mid-batch after freeze records are written but before the corresponding unfreeze records in the same file are committed, leaving TokenFreezeStatusEnum.FROZEN permanently in the token_account table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getRetry().getMaxAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a malformed record item that causes parse() to throw a retryable exception, and because retry.maxAttempts defaults to Integer.MAX_VALUE, the parser retries indefinitely on the freeze-containing file, blocking all subsequent unfreeze record files from ever being processed, resulting in permanent frozen fund state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getRetry().getMinBackoff()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where retry.minBackoff (500ms) combined with retry.maxBackoff (30s) and retry.multiplier (2) causes exponential backoff that delays processing of an unfreeze record file long enough that downstream consumers read the stale FROZEN status and permanently block fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the enabled=true default in AbstractParserProperties such that if the parser is toggled off (enabled=false) after a TOKENFREEZE record file is processed but before the TOKENUNFREEZE record file is processed, shouldParse() returns false and the unfreeze is silently skipped, permanently freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getWindow()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch.window (default 5 minutes) such that a TOKENFREEZE transaction falls within one batch window and the TOKENUNFREEZE transaction falls in the next window, and a transactionTimeout expiry during the second window's DB transaction causes the unfreeze to be rolled back while the freeze persists?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getFlushInterval()] Can an unprivileged external user time a TOKENFREEZE submission so that it is flushed to the database at the batch.flushInterval (default 2s) boundary while the TOKENUNFREEZE transaction is still buffered in memory, and a JVM crash between flushes causes the freeze to be persisted but the unfreeze to be lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getMaxFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit batch.maxFiles (default 1) such that a record file containing only a TOKENFREEZE is processed as a single-file batch and committed, while a subsequent record file containing the TOKENUNFREEZE is rejected due to a hash chain mismatch triggered by a crafted record, leaving the account permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getQueueCapacity()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the record stream to fill the batch.queueCapacity (default 10) queue with freeze-containing files, causing back-pressure that drops or delays unfreeze-containing files, resulting in a window where the mirror node's token_account table shows FROZEN status indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency (default 20ms) polling interval such that a TOKENFREEZE record file is picked up and committed in one poll cycle, but a network disruption between poll cycles prevents the TOKENUNFREEZE file from being downloaded, leaving the freeze state permanent in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the processingTimeout (default 10s) health check to trigger a parser restart after a TOKENFREEZE record file is committed but before the TOKENUNFREEZE file is processed, and if the restart resets internal state without re-queuing the unfreeze file, the account remains permanently frozen?
